questionText: A museum note says a robot show starts in the 3 o'clock hour in the afternoon. The show starts after 3:30 p.m. but before 3:45 p.m. In 24-hour time, the minutes are the difference between 50 and 13. Which 24-hour time matches the note?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can convert between 12-hour and 24-hour time
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Time Problem Solving: 24-Hour Time, aligned to Students can convert between 12-hour and 24-hour time.
explanation: The show is in the 3 o'clock hour in the afternoon, so the hour is 3 p.m. In 24-hour time, 3 p.m. becomes 15. The minutes are the difference between 50 and 13: 50 - 13 = 37. That gives 15:37. This also fits the clue that the time is after 3:30 p.m. but before 3:45 p.m. 03:37 is in the morning, 15:27 has the wrong minutes and is before 3:30 p.m., and 15:47 is after 3:45 p.m.

questionText: A camp leader wrote this claim: "11:40 a.m. and 23:40 are 12 hours apart, so they are not the same time. If another roast starts 20 minutes after 23:40, it will begin on the next day." Which option correctly judges the whole claim?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can convert between 12-hour and 24-hour time
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Time Problem Solving: 24-Hour Time, aligned to Students can convert between 12-hour and 24-hour time.
options: [{"text":"The whole claim is true.","correct":true},{"text":"Only the first sentence is true; 20 minutes after 23:40 is 23:60 on the same day.","correct":false},{"text":"Only the second sentence is true; 11:40 a.m. and 23:40 are the same time.","correct":false},{"text":"Both sentences are false because 23:40 means 1:40 p.m.","correct":false}]
explanation: 23:40 in 24-hour time is 11:40 p.m. That is 12 hours after 11:40 a.m., so the two times are not the same. Also, 20 minutes after 23:40 is 00:00, which is the start of the next day. So both parts of the claim are true.

options: [{"text":"Asha: They match because 17:09 is 5:09 p.m., and changing 5:09 p.m. back to 24-hour time gives 17:09.","correct":true},{"text":"Ben: They do not match because 17:09 means 7:09 p.m.","correct":false},{"text":"Cara: They do not match because 5:09 p.m. changes to 05:09 in 24-hour time.","correct":false},{"text":"Dev: They match because every time with 17 in front is the same as a time with 7 in front.","correct":false}]

questionText: A toy shop sign must show the 24-hour time for 9:48 p.m. Which method is correct for 9:48 p.m. and also still works for 8:48 p.m., 11:48 p.m., and 12:48 p.m.?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can convert between 12-hour and 24-hour time
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Time Problem Solving: 24-Hour Time, aligned to Students can convert between 12-hour and 24-hour time.
options: [{"text":"Add 12 to the hour for every p.m. time, so 9:48 p.m. becomes 21:48 and 12:48 p.m. becomes 24:48.","correct":false},{"text":"Keep the hour the same for all p.m. times, so 9:48 p.m. becomes 09:48.","correct":false},{"text":"Add 12 to the hour for p.m. times from 1:00 p.m. to 11:59 p.m., but keep 12:xx p.m. as 12:xx, so 9:48 p.m. becomes 21:48.","correct":true},{"text":"Change every evening time to start with 19, so 9:48 p.m. becomes 19:48.","correct":false}]
explanation: The correct rule is: for p.m. times from 1:00 p.m. to 11:59 p.m., add 12 to the hour. So 9:48 p.m. becomes 21:48, 8:48 p.m. becomes 20:48, and 11:48 p.m. becomes 23:48. But 12:48 p.m. stays 12:48 because noon times do not add 12. That is why the third option is correct. The first option fails at 12:48 p.m. because 24:48 is not correct. The second ignores p.m. completely, and the fourth uses one fixed starting number, which does not work.
